name: Charon
description: TrstLyr Protocol — trust infrastructure for the agent internet. Evaluates AI agents, skills, and repos via multi-signal trust scoring aggregated from GitHub, ERC-8004, Twitter, ClawHub, and Moltbook. Anchors results on-chain as EAS attestations on Base Mainnet. Exposes trust decisions as MCP tools and an HTTP API with x402 micropayment gating.

supported_tools.name: trust_query
supported_tools.description: Evaluate trust score for an agent, skill, or GitHub repo. Accepts namespaced subjects: github:user, github:user/repo, erc8004:id, clawhub:skill/slug, twitter:handle, moltbook:handle.

supported_tools.name: trust_gate
supported_tools.description: Pre-action trust check returning proceed:true/false with score, risk level, and threshold context. Used before trades, delegations, or agent-to-agent calls.

supported_tools.name: trust_batch
supported_tools.description: Batch trust evaluation — evaluate up to 20 subjects in a single call.

supported_tools.name: identity_register
supported_tools.description: Register and cross-verify agent identities across namespaces (GitHub, Twitter, ERC-8004, wallet). Dual-proof identity linking with cryptographic verification.

supported_tools.name: attest
supported_tools.description: Anchor trust scores as EAS attestations on Base Mainnet. First attestation free; subsequent via x402 micropayment ($0.01 USDC).

supported_tools.name: discover
supported_tools.description: Protocol-agnostic agent discovery — search and rank agents by capability, protocol, and live trust score across ERC-8004, A2A, MCP, ACP, ClawHub, Moltbook, and HOL.org.

safety_guardrails: All write operations (EAS attestation) require x402 micropayment after first free use — prevents abuse
Input size limits enforced (subject: 256 chars, payment header: 4096 bytes)
SVG output XSS-sanitized via escapeXml helper
Request deduplication via in-flight Map — prevents redundant scoring under load
Non-fatal EAS writes: scoring always returns even if on-chain write fails
Security headers: HSTS, CSP, X-Frame-Options on all responses

autonomous_capabilities.discovers_problems: Monitors provider health and degrades gracefully when data sources are unavailable
autonomous_capabilities.plans_solutions: Fuses multi-provider signals using Subjective Logic CBF — mathematically principled uncertainty handling
autonomous_capabilities.executes_tasks: Fetches from 5 providers in parallel, scores, optionally attests on-chain, returns decision
autonomous_capabilities.verifies_outputs: Confidence values (0-1) on every score; risk levels gate proceed/reject automatically
autonomous_capabilities.self_corrects: Provider failures trigger 0.5 prior fallback per Subjective Logic — score remains valid under uncertainty
